reasoning: The Biden administration's January 2025 rule (the "AI Diffusion Rule"), which introduced broad new controls on AI model weights and expanded chip controls, was rescinded/paused by the incoming Trump administration's Bureau of Industry and Security (BIS) on May 13, 2025. This initially suggests a deregulatory stance that pushes the probability of "new or expanded" controls down. However, on the same day, BIS issued strong new guidance interpreting existing "catch-all" provisions to functionally implement new restrictions—specifically on foreign Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS/cloud) providers training AI models for countries in Group D:5. Furthermore, law firms (e.g., Gibson Dunn) report that BIS explicitly announced "plans to introduce a replacement" regulatory framework. Given the ongoing strategic competition with China (which enjoys broad bipartisan support) and the historic cadence of annual export control updates (2022, 2023, 2025), it is highly credible that BIS will propose and finalize a replacement framework for AI export controls before December 31, 2025. Nevertheless, whether these controls will be structured as "broad" enough to meet the resolution criteria, or whether they will rely entirely on targeted mechanisms like the Entity List, remains somewhat uncertain. I assign a 43% probability, recognizing the high likelihood of a replacement rule but the realistic risk of it taking effect after Dec 31, 2025.
